label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's think through this step by step. The question is asking whether I end up back at the starting point after following these instructions. The instructions are: 1. Always face forward. 2. Take 2 steps backward. 3. Take 3 steps backward. 4. Take 3 steps backward. 5. Take 8 steps forward. First, I need to clarify the directions. Since "always face forward" is specified, taking steps backward would mean moving in the direction opposite to where I'm facing, right? So if I'm facing forward, moving backward would take me away from the starting point in the opposite direction of where I'm facing. Now let's track my position step by step, using a simple coordinate system where I start at position 0 and facing forward is the positive direction. Moving backward would then be in the negative direction. 1. Start at position 0. 2. Take 2 steps backward: 0 - 2 = -2. 3. Take 3 steps backward: -2 - 3 = -5. 4. Take 3 steps backward: -5 - 3 = -8. 5. Take 8 steps forward: -8 + 8 = 0. So according to this calculation, I end up back at position 0, which is the starting point. So the answer would be "Yes" I return to the starting point. Let me double-check to make sure I didn't make any errors in reasoning. Starting at 0, taking 2 steps back to -2, then 3 more to -5, then 3 more to -8, then 8 steps forward to 0. Yes, that seems to be correct. Another way to look at it is to add up all the movements: -2 (from first backward step) -3 (from second backward step) -3 (from third backward step) +8 (from forward steps) Total = -2 -3 -3 +8 =
